contextBlurb: Nova Scotia's housing policy is guided by the 'Our Homes, Action for Housing Plan,' a five-year plan targeting 41,200 new units—a goal already surpassed with 68,841 units enabled as of September 2025. Key legislative tools include the Housing Supply and Services Act and the Housing in Halifax Regional Municipality Act, both amended in February 2026 to accelerate supply and extend the Executive Panel on Housing. The province has also enacted short-term rental registration regulations and invested over $283 million in affordable housing since 2021.

legislation.billCode: Housing Supply and Services Act (2026 amendment)
legislation.title: Province Strengthens Housing Legislation to Accelerate Supply – Housing Supply and Services Act Changes
legislation.summary: Amendments give the Minister of Housing new authority to prevent delays in housing projects and reduce barriers to housing supply across Nova Scotia.

legislation.billCode: Housing in Halifax Regional Municipality Act (2026 amendment)
legislation.title: Province Strengthens Housing Legislation to Accelerate Supply – Housing in Halifax Regional Municipality Act Changes
legislation.summary: Extends the Executive Panel on Housing in the Halifax Regional Municipality to continue overseeing and expediting housing approvals in the region.

legislation.title: Housing Supply and Services Act
legislation.summary: Grants the Minister of Housing new authority to prevent delays in housing projects and accelerate housing supply across Nova Scotia.

legislation.billCode: Housing in Halifax Regional Municipality Act (amended 2026)
legislation.title: Housing in Halifax Regional Municipality Act
legislation.summary: Extends the mandate of the Executive Panel on Housing in the Halifax Regional Municipality to continue overseeing and accelerating housing development in HRM.

legislation.title: Housing Supply and Services Act
legislation.summary: Provides the legislative framework for housing supply initiatives in Nova Scotia; amended in February 2026 to give the Minister of Housing new authority to prevent delays in housing projects.

legislation.billCode: Housing in Halifax Regional Municipality Act
legislation.title: Housing in Halifax Regional Municipality Act
legislation.summary: Establishes the Executive Panel on Housing in the Halifax Regional Municipality; amended in February 2026 to extend the panel's mandate and reduce barriers to housing approvals.

legislation.title: Province Invests in Generational Affordable Housing for Shannon Park
legislation.summary: A $300-million joint federal-provincial investment to build 1,430 new homes, with 930 located at Shannon Park in Dartmouth, representing a major affordable housing commitment announced December 2025.

legislation.title: Province Invests in Generational Affordable Housing for Shannon Park
legislation.summary: A $300 million joint federal-provincial investment to build 1,430 new homes, with 930 located at Shannon Park in Dartmouth, as part of the provincial affordable housing strategy.

legislation.billCode: Our Homes, Action for Housing Plan
legislation.title: Our Homes, Action for Housing Plan – Five-Year Housing Strategy
legislation.summary: A five-year government plan with 12 key actions to increase housing supply, improve affordability, and use provincial land and infrastructure to create housing across Nova Scotia; as of July 2025, 515 new rent-geared-to-income public housing units have been committed.

legislation.title: Rent-Geared-to-Income Public Housing Program
legislation.summary: Since 2023, the Province has committed to building 515 new rent-geared-to-income public housing units across Nova Scotia, providing homes for more than 1,300 people.

legislation.title: Rent-Geared-to-Income Public Housing Units Program
legislation.summary: Since 2023, the Province has committed to building 515 new rent-geared-to-income public housing units across Nova Scotia to provide homes for more than 1,300 residents.

legislation.title: Community Housing Acquisition Program
legislation.summary: Provides low-interest mortgages and funding to community organizations to purchase and preserve affordable housing properties; a $1.3-million mortgage was recently issued to Open Arms Resource Centre in Kentville.

legislation.title: Short-term Rentals Registration Regulations
legislation.summary: Requires all short-term accommodations offering stays of 28 consecutive days or less to register to operate in Nova Scotia, with an appeals process for enforcement actions.

legislation.title: Short-term Rentals Registration Regulations
legislation.summary: Requires all short-term accommodations (28 consecutive days or less) to register to operate in Nova Scotia, with an appeal process for enforcement actions under section 11.

legislation.title: Rent Supplement: Canada-Nova Scotia Targeted Housing Benefit
legislation.summary: A joint federal-provincial program providing rent supplements to help low-income renters with housing costs in Nova Scotia.

legislation.title: Affordable Housing Development Process
legislation.summary: A government guide outlining the housing development process and funding eligibility criteria for private and community housing developers seeking provincial funding to create affordable housing.
